executive_summary: The global network exhibits moderate instability characterised by elevated energy sector volatility in the Middle East and acute psychological agitation in the Taiwan Strait. Whilst physical supply chains and fuel reserves remain operational, the discrepancy between media alarmism and actual physical movement suggests active cognitive warfare. Speculative pressure within the technology sector further compounding systemic vulnerability requires close monitoring.
correlations: The divergence between high Taiwanese media panic and negative physical change suggests that informational manipulation is being deployed to preemptively stress global supply chains prior to any physical disruption.
